key_themes: Shareholder dilution from repeated placings and fundraisings
Delays and extensions on proposed Gulf International Minerals acquisition
Operational and diamond updates from Zimbabwe assets
Recent trading suspension and corporate restructuring
High volatility in a microcap mining stock
summary: Vast Resources (VAST.L), a UK-listed microcap miner with African assets, shows predominantly negative sentiment driven by chronic cash burn, multiple discounted placings that heavily dilute shareholders, a trading suspension in February 2026, and repeated extensions on a key acquisition (now to May 2026). Operational updates on Zimbabwe diamonds and mining provide occasional positive spikes and keep speculative retail interest alive on busy forums like ADVFN, but the overall picture is one of caution, frustration, and high risk of further downside in this volatile penny stock currently trading near 0.12p with a ~£6M market cap.
sources: Yahoo Finance (10+ recent news items and RNS), LSE RNS announcements (suspension Feb 2026, acquisition updates Mar 2026), ADVFN Vast Resources share chat (>108,000 posts, very active retail discussion), Proactive Investors RNS feeds, X/Twitter (~5-10 relevant posts found, low volume)
